metadata.title: Narrative Report -- Turnbull Refuge -- July 16 to Oct. 31, 1938

pages.raw_ocr_text:
1. General
31 days with temperatures of 90 degrees or above. Only .55 inches of rain fell.
All water places dried up during August except Highbridge Pothole and Pine Creek.

A fire on July 16 burned 100 acres and killed about 100 cords of wood, with suppression cost of $52.50.
Another fire on Sept. 4 burned 80 acres with suppression cost of $7.00.

2. Wildlife
Approximately 900 mallards were reared to maturity and 100 green-wing teal were reared.
500 coot were reared and about 3000 mallards came into the refuge during hunting season.
700 Canada geese came into Turnbull Refuge.

3. Economic uses
No grazing allowed this year. Haying by 6 farmers under permits produced 173.5 tons on 175 acres and revenue of $347.00.
